title: Amphibious Seismic Survey Images Plate Interface at 1960 Chile Earthquake
abstract: The southern central Chilean margin at the site of the largest historically recorded earthquake (Valdivia 1960, Mw=9.5) was investigated using an offshore-onshore seismic survey conducted in 2001 during SPOC (Subduction Processes Off Chile, R/V SONNE SO-161). Seismic data examine the subduction zone lithospheric structure and margin evolution over the 36–40°S segment. Both marine wide-angle OBS seismic refraction lines and near-vertical seismic reflection profiles were acquired, along with an onshore component. The combined dataset images the plate interface, accretionary prism, and subducting Nazca plate geometry at the site of the great 1960 earthquake.

location.description: Offshore south-central Chile (36°–40°S) at the site of the 1960 Mw 9.5 Valdivia earthquake

data.name: SPOC Marine Wide-angle OBS Seismic Refraction Data (SO161)
data.description: Marine wide-angle OBS seismic refraction data from SPOC experiment (R/V SONNE SO161, 2001), covering 36°–40°S offshore south-central Chile. Foundational dataset for this region.

data.name: SPOC Near-vertical MCS Reflection Data (SO161)
data.description: Near-vertical incidence MCS reflection profiles from SPOC experiment (SO161) imaging the plate interface, accretionary prism, and fore-arc structure at 36°–40°S.

processing.summary: This Eos paper presents preliminary results from the SPOC (SO161) amphibious campaign at the 1960 earthquake site. Data reported include marine wide-angle OBS refraction and near-vertical MCS reflection profiles. As a short communication paper, it reports early-stage processing results (stacked sections, first velocity models). Full processing and interpretation published in subsequent papers (e.g., Contreras-Reyes 2008, Scherwath 2009).
workflow: 1. SPOC SO161 marine and land data acquisition (2001)
processing.notes: Eos short communication reporting early SPOC results. Bronze open access PDF available. Many authors are GFZ Potsdam researchers.

analysis_notes: Bronze open access PDF available via Wiley. SPOC SO161 cruise (2001) confirmed from authorship and companion papers. This is a short Eos communication reporting preliminary results; full analysis in later papers. Data held at GEOMAR and GFZ Potsdam.
